title: Tiruvaaippadi
tagline: Tiruvaaippadi — spiritual town of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: The Navamohana Krishna Temple, also referred to in Sri Vaishnava tradition as Tiruvaipadi, is one of the Divya Desams, the 108 temples of Vishnu revered by the 12 poet-saints calle
overview.description: The Navamohana Krishna Temple, also referred to in Sri Vaishnava tradition as Tiruvaipadi, is one of the Divya Desams, the 108 temples of Vishnu revered by the 12 poet-saints called the Alvars. It is located in modern day Gokul, in the Mathura district of the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is located 15 km south-east of Mathura. It is regarded to be the site of Krishna's childhood past-times.

topPlaces.name: Gokul
topPlaces.description: Gokul is a town in the Mathura district of the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is located 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) south-east of Mathura.

topPlaces.name: Gokul barrage
topPlaces.description: The Gokul barrage, also Mathura barrage is a barrage on Yamuna River at Gokul in Mathura district, top of which also serves as the road bridge. Yamuna has a total of 6 barrages, from north-west to south-east, Dakpathar Barrage (Uttarakhand), Hathni Kund Barrage (172km from Yamunotri origin, replaced the older defunct Tajewala Barrage in Haryana), Wazirabad barrage (244km from Hathnikund to north Delhi), ITO barrage (central Delhi), Okhla barrage (22km from Wazirabad to south Delhi, "New Okhla barrage" is later-era new barrage) and Mathura barrage (at Gokul, Uttar Pradesh).

topPlaces.name: Mirzapur stele inscription
topPlaces.description: The Mirzapur stele inscription, also called the Mirjāpur stele inscription, is a dedicatory inscription on a large stone slab discovered in the Mirzapur area of Mathura which mentions the erection of a water tank by Mulavasu and his consort Kausiki, during the reign of the Sodasa, the Indo-Scythian Northern Satrap ruler of Mathura, assuming the title of "Svami (Lord) Mahakshatrapa (Great Satrap)".

topPlaces.name: Aurangabad Bangar
topPlaces.description: Aurangabad Bangar is a census town in Mathura district in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Mahaban
topPlaces.description: Mahaban is a town and a nagar panchayat in Mathura district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Sacred Heart Catholic Church, Mathura
topPlaces.description: The Sacred Heart Catholic Church is an architecturally notable church in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India. The design combines Gothic principles, Indian craftsmanship, and a Russian dome.

topPlaces.name: Bad, Uttar Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Baad is a census town in Mathura district in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. It is located on NH2, near Indian Oil corporation and Baad railway station, Mathura refinery Township.

topPlaces.name: Government Museum, Mathura
topPlaces.description: Government Museum, Mathura, commonly referred to as Mathura museum, is an archaeological museum in Mathura city of Uttar Pradesh state in India. The museum was founded by then collector of the Mathura district, Sir F. S. Growse in 1874.

items.note: Gokul is a town in the Mathura district of the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is located 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) south-east of Mathura.

The Gokul barrage, also Mathura barrage is a barrage on Yamuna River at Gokul in Mathura district, top of which also serves as the road bridge. Yamuna has a total of 6 barrages, from north-west to south-east, Dakpathar Barrage (Uttarakhand), Hathni Kund Barrage (172km from Yamunotri origin, replaced the older defunct Tajewala Barrage in Haryana), Wazirabad barrage (244km from Hathnikund to north Delhi), ITO barrage (central Delhi), Okhla barrage (22km from Wazirabad to south Delhi, "New Okhla barrage" is later-era new barrage) and Mathura barrage (at Gokul, Uttar Pradesh).

The Mirzapur stele inscription, also called the Mirjāpur stele inscription, is a dedicatory inscription on a large stone slab discovered in the Mirzapur area of Mathura which mentions the erection of a water tank by Mulavasu and his consort Kausiki, during the reign of the Sodasa, the Indo-Scythian Northern Satrap ruler of Mathura, assuming the title of "Svami (Lord) Mahakshatrapa (Great Satrap)".
Aurangabad Bangar is a census town in Mathura district in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India.
Mahaban is a town and a nagar panchayat in Mathura district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.
The Sacred Heart Catholic Church is an architecturally notable church in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India. The design combines Gothic principles, Indian craftsmanship, and a Russian dome.
Baad is a census town in Mathura district in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. It is located on NH2, near Indian Oil corporation and Baad railway station, Mathura refinery Township.

Government Museum, Mathura, commonly referred to as Mathura museum, is an archaeological museum in Mathura city of Uttar Pradesh state in India. The museum was founded by then collector of the Mathura district, Sir F. S. Growse in 1874.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tiruvaaippadi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tiruvaaippadi is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

The nearest airport is Agra Airport (Pandit Deen Dayal Upadhyay) (41 km) and the nearest railway station is Agra Cantt (42 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tiruvaaippadi?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tiruvaaippadi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tiruvaaippadi?
faq.a: Stays in Tiruvaaippadi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tiruvaaippadi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gokul, Gokul barrage, Mirzapur stele inscription, Aurangabad Bangar, Mahaban.
